display.purpose: Hero headline only — one per page.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked eyebrows, tags, and spec keys — the technical stamp.

spec.purpose: Tabular mono for spec values and animated counters.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Detached glass nav pill at rest.

shadows.name: lift
shadows.purpose: Cards and imagery on hover, paired with translateY(-6px).

shadows.name: accent-glow
shadows.purpose: Under a primary orange button on hover only.

motion.name: ease-mechanical
motion.purpose: Confident spring-like easing for hovers and reveals.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The lead-generating CTA. Used once per section, max.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action (e.g. 'Ver ficha técnica'). Hairline that goes orange on hover.

components.name: Mono Eyebrow
components.description: The recurring technical stamp above section titles.

components.name: Equipment Tag
components.description: Category label pinned over equipment photography.

components.name: Spec Tile
components.description: Signature pattern — turns a dry spec into a scannable datasheet cell.

components.name: Product Card
components.description: Flat at rest; lifts and dissolves its border on hover.
components.html:
Construcción y Minería

Excavadoras, cargadores, dozers y camiones para superficie y subterránea.

components.name: Glass Nav Pill
components.description: Floating, detached, blurred — carries the persistent CTA.

components.name: Email Input
components.description: Lead-capture field; border goes orange on focus.

narrative.overview: Maquinarias Mega sells multi-hundred-thousand-dollar iron to people who read spec sheets for a living. The interface should feel like a precisely engineered technical document made physical: every number legible and tabular, every edge aligned to a grid, every surface machined to fit. It borrows the photographic authority of Komatsu and Caterpillar and marries it to the calm, white-space-rich restraint of ABB and Siemens. The result reads as expensive because it is exact, not because it is loud. It rejects the generic Bootstrap/AI-template look, sterile corporate stock, and playful consumer-startup aesthetics alike.
keyCharacteristics: Precision-grid alignment and generous macro-whitespace (~120px sections)
A single disciplined orange accent on a carbon-and-mist neutral field
Monospaced technical type for specs, labels, and counters — data as the hero
Real equipment photography carrying the emotional weight; UI stays out of its way

rules.name: The One Orange Rule
rules.body: Orange covers ≤10% of any viewport — one primary action per section, one key word per headline. Its scarcity is what makes it read as 'the next step.'

rules.name: The No-Pure-Black Rule
rules.body: Never #000. Carbon (#14171A) is the floor — it reads as forged steel, not cheap flat black.

rules.name: The Tabular Numbers Rule
rules.body: Every comparable number is mono with tabular-nums, so digits align and never jitter as counters animate.

rules.name: The Mono-Stamp Rule
rules.body: Eyebrows and tags are always uppercase IBM Plex Mono with 0.16em tracking — the system's technical signature.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest; shadow is earned by interaction or genuine elevation (the floating nav).

rules.name: The Ghost-Border Rule
rules.body: Borders are 1px Line 200 at most and dissolve on hover into a Lift shadow. Never a colored or >1px decorative border.

dos: Do keep orange to ≤10% of any viewport — one primary action, one key word.
Do set every comparable number in IBM Plex Mono with tabular-nums.
Do let real equipment photography carry the impact; keep the UI restrained around it.
Do build depth from spacing and tonal layering first; reserve soft shadows for hover and the floating nav.
Do give every section one obvious next step toward a lead, with trust signals nearby.
Do use carbon (#14171A) as the darkest value, never #000.
Do float the nav as a glass pill with macro-whitespace; let sections breathe at ~120px.
donts: Don't ship the generic Bootstrap / 'AI template' look — no cookie-cutter 3-column cards, default system fonts, harsh 1px gray borders, or cramped grids.
Don't use sterile corporate stock imagery or empty buzzwords — show real iron and real operations.
Don't go playful / consumer — no bubbly radii, pastels, or startup-cute illustration.
Don't let orange compete with itself; two oranges in one glance means one is wrong.
Don't use a colored or >1px border as decoration — hairline Line 200, dissolving on hover.
Don't convey state by color alone — pair active/error states with text or iconography.
Don't use harsh dark drop shadows; soft and diffuse only.
